title: task
description:
python/src/lichen/radio/sim_client.py:128-134 - If registration fails (MSG_ERR or unexpected response), the TCP stream is not closed before raising. The stream remains connected but unusable, leaking the socket until GC.

Failure scenario: When the simulator rejects registration (e.g., duplicate node_id, invalid sim_id), connect() raises SimRadioError but leaves self._stream set to the open socket. The caller cannot use the SimRadio instance (registration failed) but the socket remains open, consuming OS resources until garbage collection.

Fix: Close the stream before raising in error paths.
close_reason: Fixed: _recv() now calls aclose() before setting self._stream = None on protocol errors, preventing socket leak when cleanup handler checks stream is not None
